title: clavija procera
intro: Clavija procera, also known as the "Kawakawa" or "Piper excelsum," is a tropical plant native to the Pacific Islands, particularly in New Zealand and other Polynesian islands. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Clavija procera include reducing pain and inflammation, treating fever, and supporting digestive health. Clavija procera is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or mixing them with a carrier oil.
